evidence: sdk-bridge.ts is 917 LOC — combines type definitions, interface contracts, helper functions (sanitizeUrl, encodeHivePayload, decodeHiveData, scanFilteredTransactions), and the 470+ line createSdkBridge factory. The factory returns an object literal with 15+ methods, each containing substantial logic.

action-executor.ts ActionExecutorContext has 22 fields (identity, auth, budget, config, LLM, mutable state, platform abstractions, rate limiting, telemetry) — mixing too many concerns in one context interface
lifecycle-plugin.ts uses 7 `any` types in 95 lines — catalog sources cast to any[], transitions typed as any[], updatedMap as Map<string, any>
engage-heuristics.ts uses any[] for remainingPosts and any for post parameter — known post shapes exist (ScanPost)
source-discovery.ts has 4 explicit any types including extractTitles closure and catch blocks
Positive: toolkit tools follow a consistent pattern — Zod validation + withToolWrapper + typed ToolResult
Positive: agent-config.ts cleanly separates resolution, path building, and loading into distinct functions

6 files share smells + structural detector pattern (agent-config, signal-detection, catalog, matcher, declarative-engine, sdk-bridge) — all are large files (571-1534 LOC) serving as domain complexity concentrators

design_coherence.dimension_character: The toolkit layer demonstrates strong design coherence — tools follow a consistent pattern (Zod schema validation, withToolWrapper, typed ToolResult), and the session/guard/bridge separation is clean. However, the src/lib and src/actions layers show mixed coherence: sdk-bridge.ts accumulates too many responsibilities in a single 917-line factory, lifecycle-plugin.ts uses pervasive `any` despite available typed alternatives, and action-executor.ts has a 22-field context interface mixing identity, auth, budget, LLM, and telemetry concerns. Several `any` types persist in engage-heuristics.ts and source-discovery.ts where typed alternatives exist.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The toolkit layer's consistent tool pattern and clean type contracts pull the score up, but sdk-bridge.ts as a 917-line concentration point, the 22-field ActionExecutorContext, and persistent any types across 4+ files in the lib layer create real design friction. This sits below the 80 anchor because a developer modifying sdk-bridge.ts or action-executor.ts faces tangled responsibilities, and the any types undermine the typed contracts established elsewhere.

issues.summary: sdk-bridge.ts (917 LOC) combines type definitions, helpers, and a 15-method factory in one file
evidence: File contains: 7 interface/type definitions, 5 standalone helper functions (sanitizeUrl, encodeHivePayload, decodeHiveData, scanFilteredTransactions, extractTxHash), and a createSdkBridge factory returning 15+ methods
The HIVE encoding/decoding helpers and transaction scanning are reusable primitives that don't need the bridge context
issues.suggestion: Extract HIVE encoding/decoding helpers (encodeHivePayload, decodeHiveData, scanFilteredTransactions) into a dedicated src/toolkit/hive-codec.ts. This keeps sdk-bridge.ts focused on the bridge factory and its methods. The type definitions can stay since they define the bridge's contract.

issues.summary: lifecycle-plugin.ts uses 7 `any` types in 95 lines despite typed catalog types existing
evidence: Line 39: `const allSources = catalog.sources as any[]` — SourceRecordV2[] exists
Line 49-50: `const transitions: any[]` and `const updatedMap = new Map<string, any>()`
Line 68: `(s: any)` in map callback
Line 78: `catch (e: any)` instead of `catch (e: unknown)`
issues.suggestion: Import SourceRecordV2 from catalog.ts and type allSources, updatedMap, and transitions properly. Use `catch (e: unknown)` with toErrorMessage() pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.

issues.summary: engage-heuristics.ts uses `any` for post types where ScanPost exists
evidence: Line 28: `remainingPosts: any[]` in EnforceDisagreeInput interface
Line 47: `post: any` parameter — ScanPost type from toolkit/types.ts describes this shape
issues.suggestion: Import ScanPost from toolkit/types.ts and replace `any[]` with `ScanPost[]` and `any` with `ScanPost`. The comment on line 10 says it avoids SDK import chain — ScanPost is in toolkit/types.ts which has no SDK deps.

issues.summary: source-discovery.ts has 4 explicit `any` types in typed domain code
evidence: Line 125: `const extractTitles = (obj: any)` — extracting titles from API response
Lines 281, 343: `catch (err: any)` — should use unknown + type narrowing
Line 324: `let parsed: any` — YAML parse result has known shape
issues.suggestion: Type extractTitles parameter as `Record<string, unknown>` with nested access guards. Use `catch (e: unknown)` with toErrorMessage(). Type the YAML parse result with the known source registry shape.

issues.summary: attestation-executor.ts (119 LOC) is orphaned with zero importers
evidence: File has zero importers — not referenced by any other module, not a CLI entry point, not dynamically imported
issues.suggestion: Delete the file if it is truly unused. If it is planned for future use, add a TODO comment explaining when it will be wired in.

issues.summary: 6 files (571-1534 LOC) share structural complexity pattern without clear extraction opportunities
evidence: All 6 files triggered both smells and structural detectors
declarative-engine.ts is 1534 LOC — the largest non-test file
sdk-bridge.ts is 917 LOC combining types, helpers, and factory
issues.suggestion: For sdk-bridge.ts, extract HIVE codec helpers. For declarative-engine.ts, evaluate whether spec validation and adapter generation can be separated. The other files (agent-config, catalog, matcher, signal-detection) are large because their domains are genuinely complex — no forced extraction recommended.

root_causes: Persistent `any` types in 4+ files undermine the typed contracts established in the toolkit layer
sdk-bridge.ts accumulated responsibilities over multiple migration iterations without periodic extraction
likely_symptoms: lifecycle_plugin_any_types and engage_heuristics_any are symptoms of incomplete type migration

add.description: All 10 toolkit tools use the same structure: Zod schema validation, withToolWrapper for lifecycle, typed ToolResult<T> return. A developer can learn one tool and predict the shape of all others.
At 917 LOC, sdk-bridge.ts combines type definitions, HIVE encoding helpers, transaction scanning utilities, and a 15-method factory. It grew through chain-first migration without periodic extraction.

add.header: Persistent any types in lib layer
add.description: 4+ files in src/lib/ and src/plugins/ use explicit `any` types where typed alternatives exist (ScanPost, SourceRecordV2, unknown). The toolkit layer is well-typed but the lib layer has gaps.

add.header: Large files serve domain complexity, not mixing
add.description: Files like catalog.ts (698 LOC), matcher.ts (644 LOC), and agent-config.ts (571 LOC) are large because their domains are genuinely complex, not because they mix unrelated concerns. Each has a single coherent responsibility.
